name: Samarkand
summary: Samarkand is one of Uzbekistan's safest tourist destinations, with low violent crime rates and excellent infrastructure. The city has dedicated tourist police and is considered significantly safer than many European destinations for travelers.
quickVerdict: Samarkand offers exceptional safety for tourists, with violent crime being extremely rare and petty theft less common than in most popular tourist destinations. The city benefits from dedicated tourist police, modern infrastructure, and a welcoming local population. While standard travel precautions apply, particularly regarding scams and night safety, Samarkand consistently ranks among the safest cities in Central Asia. Women report feeling very safe traveling solo, and families find the city highly accommodating.

neighborhoods.name: Historic Center & Registan
neighborhoods.description: The main tourist area around Registan Square is heavily patrolled with tourist police presence. Well-lit streets, numerous hotels, and constant foot traffic make this the safest area for visitors, especially at night.

neighborhoods.name: Old City
neighborhoods.description: The traditional old town area with historic monuments like Shah-i-Zinda is very safe during day and evening hours. Tourist-friendly with good police presence and well-maintained pathways.

neighborhoods.name: Central Commercial District
neighborhoods.description: Modern shopping areas and main boulevards are safe and well-policed. Good for evening dining and shopping with reliable public transportation access.

neighborhoods.name: Residential Mahallas
neighborhoods.description: Traditional neighborhood areas are generally safe but may lack tourist infrastructure. Locals are friendly and helpful, though language barriers may exist.

neighborhoods.name: Train Station Area
neighborhoods.description: Generally safe but requires normal urban caution. Some reports of overcharging by taxi drivers. Use official taxis or pre-arrange transportation.

neighborhoods.name: Outskirts & Industrial Areas
neighborhoods.description: Less developed areas with minimal tourist infrastructure. Avoid after dark and use reliable transportation when traveling through these areas.

scams.name: Fake Tourist Guides
scams.description: Unofficial guides approach tourists at major sites claiming to offer special access or skip-the-line services for inflated prices.
scams.howToAvoid: Only book tours through licensed operators, hotels, or official tourism offices. Verify guide credentials and agree on prices upfront.

scams.name: Police Extortion
scams.description: Some reports of police officers requesting small bribes for minor infractions or special access to monuments, though this has decreased significantly.
scams.howToAvoid: Carry proper documentation, be polite but firm, ask to go to the police station if questioned, and report incidents to tourist police.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Fraud
scams.description: Street money changers may offer better rates but use counterfeit bills or shortchange tourists during transactions.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official banks, hotels, or licensed exchange offices. Count money carefully and verify authenticity of bills before completing transactions.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Drivers may quote inflated prices to foreigners or claim meters are broken, especially at airports and tourist sites.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ride-sharing apps like Yandex, agree on fare before starting, or ask locals about appropriate prices for common routes.

soloFemale.overview: Samarkand is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, with many women reporting feeling safer than in European cities. The local culture is respectful, and harassment is very rare. Tourist police are easily accessible, and accommodation options cater well to solo travelers.
tips: Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees to show cultural respect
Carry a headscarf for entering active mosques
Use official taxis or ride-sharing apps rather than accepting unsolicited transport offers
Stay in central accommodations near major tourist sites for convenience and safety
Learn basic Uzbek or Russian phrases as English is limited outside tourist areas

nightSafety.overview: Samarkand is generally safe at night, particularly in tourist areas which remain well-lit and patrolled. The city has low crime rates, but standard urban precautions are recommended. Most venues close by midnight due to local regulations.
tips: Stick to well-lit main streets and tourist areas after dark
Use reliable transportation rather than walking alone in unfamiliar neighborhoods
Be aware that most nightlife venues close by midnight or 1 AM
Avoid accepting drinks from strangers or following locals to unofficial nightlife venues
Keep emergency contacts easily accessible on your phone

transport.metro: No metro system in Samarkand. The city relies on buses, taxis, and walking for local transport.
transport.rideshare: Yandex Go is the main ride-sharing app available and is generally safe and affordable. MyTaxi is also available but less popular.
transport.taxis: Official marked taxis are safe but negotiate fares beforehand as meters are not always used. Many unlicensed taxis operate - use official services when possible.
transport.tips: High-speed Afrosiyob train to Tashkent and Bukhara is the safest intercity option. Book transport in advance during peak season and always verify driver credentials.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes and active mosques
Use right hand for greetings, eating, and passing objects
Dress modestly with shoulders and knees covered, especially in religious sites
Greet with 'Assalomu alaykum' followed by handshake
Show respect to elders by standing when they enter and offering seats
Avoid public displays of affection and loud behavior in public spaces

health.overview: Healthcare in Samarkand is improving with new facilities and digital systems, though private clinics offer better service for tourists. Major cities have adequate medical care.
health.water: Tap water is not recommended for drinking. Use bottled water or water filtration systems. Most hotels provide safe drinking water.
health.vaccinations: No specific vaccinations required for entry. Standard travel vaccines (hepatitis A/B, typhoid) recommended by some doctors.
health.altitude: Samarkand sits at relatively low elevation with hot, dry summers and cold winters. Stay hydrated in summer and dress appropriately for season.

emergency.usEmbassy: U.S. Embassy Tashkent: +998 71 120 54 50. Located at 3 Moyqorghon Street, 5th block, Yunusobod District, Tashkent 100093. Tourist hotline: 1173 (24/7 English support).

faq.a: Yes, Samarkand is very safe for tourists with low violent crime rates and good tourist infrastructure. It's considered one of the safest destinations in Central Asia with dedicated tourist police and welcoming locals.
Samarkand is generally safe at night, especially in tourist areas that remain well-lit and patrolled. Stick to main streets and use reliable transportation for maximum safety.

faq.q: Is Samarkand safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, Samarkand is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers. Many women report feeling safer than in European cities, with respectful locals and minimal harassment reported.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Samarkand?
faq.a: Avoid outskirts and industrial areas after dark, and be cautious around the train station where taxi overcharging is common. The main tourist areas are very safe.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Samarkand?
faq.a: Yes, public transport is generally safe and reliable. Buses and official taxis are the main options. Use ride-sharing apps for convenience and transparent pricing.
